text.title: mob-programming Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for mob-programming as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Tourist mob: participants not engaged, looking at phones, not contributing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Breaks the knowledge-sharing contract; use round-robin navigation and active facilitation.

failure-mode.description: Expert dominance: one person controls all decisions, others' ideas dismissed.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Facilitator must enforce round-robin; use "yes, and" rule to build on all ideas.

failure-mode.description: No retrospective: problems persist across sessions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Schedule 15 minutes of retro after every mob session; improvements are cumulative.

failure-mode.description: Over-fast driver: agent commits before navigators finish discussing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add an explicit "wait for GO keyword from facilitator" rule in the driver prompt; no commit without GO signal.

failure-mode.description: Silent rewrites: agent reformats unrelated code while "fixing one line".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Restrict agent edits to changed lines only; facilitator inspects the diff before any commit is allowed.

failure-mode.description: Lost context on rotation: switching agent from driver to navigator erases its memory of the last 7 minutes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Maintain MOB_LOG.md and feed it as the first element of every new rotation prompt.
